title: Fiber Optic Cables
description: Glass or plastic cables that transmit data as pulses of light — immune to EMI and capable of very long distances.
fullExplanation: Fiber optic cables transmit data as light pulses through a glass or plastic core. Total internal reflection keeps light inside the core. Single-mode fiber (SMF) has a tiny 9µm core — only one light path, minimal dispersion, used for long distances (up to 80km without amplification). Multi-mode fiber (MMF) has a larger 50/62.5µm core — multiple light paths, more dispersion, used for short distances (up to 550m). Fiber is immune to electromagnetic interference and supports much higher bandwidth than copper.

connectors.name: LC
connectors.description: Small form factor, most common in data centers

connectors.name: MPO/MTP
connectors.description: Multi-fiber connector (12 or 24 fibers) for high-density 40G/100G

keyFacts: Light travels at ~200,000 km/s in fiber (2/3 speed of light in vacuum)
Immune to electromagnetic interference (EMI) — ideal near power equipment
No electrical signal — cannot be tapped with inductive probes
EDFA (Erbium-Doped Fiber Amplifier) boosts signal without converting to electrical
DWDM (Dense Wavelength Division Multiplexing): 80+ wavelengths on one fiber
Bend radius: fiber breaks if bent too sharply (typically >30mm radius)
Cleanliness critical — dust on connector causes 3dB+ loss
